conditional_states.condition: Button triggers an action whose completion is worth confirming in place (e.g. Copy, Save).
The triggered action can fail and the button should reflect or allow retry.
Button acts as a toggle or segmented option with an on/off state.
Action is visible but the user lacks rights; the disabled reason must be explained.
Rendered on a coarse-pointer device requiring a 44px minimum target.
Loading spinner and success animation must have a static equivalent.

rationale: Buttons are the atomic action trigger, so the full interaction set — default, hover, focus, active (pressed), disabled, and loading — is mandatory. The loading state is required to prevent double submission and to communicate that an async action is in flight; disabled must always be visually and semantically distinct from default.
evidence: WCAG 2.2 2.4.7 (Focus Visible) and 2.5.8 (Target Size Minimum, 24px CSS / 44px touch) apply directly to buttons.
Material and Apple HIG both define pressed (active) and disabled as required button states.
NN/g: disable and show a spinner on submit buttons during async work to prevent duplicate actions.
